channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mashatile affirms 1913 as the legal basis for land restitution, rejects 1652 cut-off
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile has clarified that the South African government will not adopt 1652 as a cut-off date for land reform and restitution. Speaking in the National Assembly, Mashatile emph...

item.title: Jason Lewis returns after three-year hiatus and shares update on creative work
item.description: Jason Lewis, famed for his role as Samantha Jones' love interest on Sex And The City, has broken his three-year silence with a video posted on Instagram. The 54-year-old actor explained that he went q...

item.title: Female Nursing Student from Abia Found Dead in Imo Apartment Under Suspicious Circumstances
item.description: Panic don take over Umulogho community for Obowo Local Government Area, Imo State after the lifeless body of Wendy Achumba, a female nursing student of College of Nursing, Umuadara Umulogho, was disco...

item.title: Stephen Colbert delivers farewell monologue as The Late Show concludes after 11 years
item.description: Stephen Colbert concluded his tenure as host of The Late Show with a heartfelt and humorous final monologue on Thursday night. The 62-year-old thanked viewers for joining him over the past 11 years an...

item.title: Pedro Almodovar Speaks Out on Trump, Gaza, and Artistic Freedom at Cannes 2026
item.description: Spanish filmmaker Pedro Almodovar created a buzz at the 79th Cannes Film Festival with his outspoken remarks regarding political issues and artistic freedom. Speaking at a press conference for his lat...

item.title: Matatu Owners Choose Sakaja as Guarantor Amid Fuel Price Talks
item.description: Matatu operators in Nairobi have opened up on why they picked Governor Johnson Sakaja as a guarantor in talks with the national government over rising fuel costs that led to a transport strike. Accord...

item.title: Frank Edoho Responds to Allegations Through Legal Channels, Denies Abuse and Infidelity
item.description: Media personality Frank Edoho don respond to claims wey him estranged wife, Sandra Edoho, don make against am for domestic abuse and cheating. For Instagram post, Frank talk say all the claims wey dey...

item.title: Human Rights Activist Justice Crack Released From DSS After Four Weeks in Detention
item.description: Human rights activist and blogger Justice Mark Chidiebere, known as Justice Crack, don finally regain him freedom from DSS custody after near four weeks. Omoyele Sowore, former presidential candidate ...

item.title: Residents Displaced by Wyndham Crossing Fire Struggle to Recover Belongings
item.description: A fire at the Wyndham Crossing apartment complex in northeast Edmonton on April 20 displaced more than 200 residents, many of whom are still struggling a month later. Residents Kimberly Roe and Wendy ...

item.title: AT&T Challenges California Rules to Phase Out Copper Phone Lines
item.description: AT&T has filed a lawsuit against California officials seeking relief from the state's requirement to continue offering traditional copper wire phone service to new customers. The company argues that m...

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders report modest attendance growth in 2025 season
item.description: The Las Vegas Raiders experienced a small increase in actual attendance at Allegiant Stadium during the 2025 NFL season, despite a challenging year on the field. The team finished with a 3-14 record, ...

item.title: Ghanaian Government Coordinates Evacuation of Nationals from South Africa Following Xenophobic Violence
item.description: More than 800 Ghanaians living in South Africa have registered for evacuation due to a recent surge in xenophobic attacks. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ghana confirmed that the evacuation, origi...

item.title: Arbitrator Rules Air Transat Cabin Crew Prohibited from Using Cannabis On or Off Duty
item.description: An arbitrator has ruled that Air Transat cabin crew members are prohibited from consuming cannabis both during work hours and in their personal time. The decision follows a dispute between the airline...

item.title: Waymo Temporarily Halts Robotaxi Service in Four Cities Due to Flooding Risks
item.description: Waymo has temporarily suspended its robotaxi operations in four U.S. cities—Atlanta, San Antonio, Dallas, and Houston—after its autonomous vehicles struggled with flooded roads during heavy rainfall. ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif to undertake official visit to China from May 23-26
item.description: According to an official statement from the Foreign Office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif is scheduled to visit China from May 23 to May 26 upon the formal invitation of the...

item.title: Rumuokparali Monarch Warns Community About Attempted Leadership Takeover
item.description: Eze Onyema Dike-Nene, di Paramount Ruler of Rumuokparali Clan for Obio/Akpor LGA, don raise alarm say some people for di community dey try frame am and him family to remove am from di throne. For one ...

item.title: French Open Maintains Prize Money Despite Player Complaints
item.description: The French Open organizers have confirmed that there will be no changes to the tournament’s prize money this year, despite growing dissatisfaction from top tennis players. Tournament director Amelie M...

item.title: US Senators Propose Review of Tanzania Relations and Suspend Aid Pending Democratic Reforms
item.description: On 19th May 2026, U.S. senators Jeanne Shaheen (Democrat) and Ted Cruz (Republican) tabled the bipartisan Reassessing the United States-Tanzania Bilateral Relationship Act. The bill calls for a detail...

item.title: Prasa unions demand urgent talks over retrenchment affecting 600 staff
item.description: The two major unions at the Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa (Prasa), the United National Transport Union (Untu) and the SA Transport and Allied Workers Union (Satawu), have called for urgent tal...

item.title: US Imposes Travel Ban on Tanzanian Police Officer Over Human Rights Concerns
item.description: The United States government has placed travel restrictions on Tanzanian police officer Faustine Mafwele, following credible reports linking him to serious human rights abuses. Secretary of State Marc...
